Thai fish cake with sweet chilli dipping sauce

english: Thai fish cake with sweet chilli dipping sauce
malay: Cekodok Ikan Goreng Thai (Tod Man Pla)
thai: ทอดมันปลา (Tod Mun Pla)

food_subgroup: Deep-fried minced fish paste patties with red curry paste, kaffir lime leaves, fish sauce, and green beans, served with sweet chilli dipping sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no dedicated concept for Thai Fish Cake (Tod Mun Pla). Closest parent is a deep-fried fish paste product. No URI assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Deep-fried Thai fish cake patties (minced fish, red curry paste, kaffir lime leaves, green beans) with sweet chilli dipping sauce
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GPF is the closest FoodEx2 parent for deep-fried fish paste products; no exact code exists for Tod Mun Pla specifically.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept identified for Thai fish cake; code left null to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Tod Mun Pla is 4 fish cake patties (approximately 100g) served with 30g sweet chilli dipping sauce, totalling 130g.

nutrient_source.title: Tod Mun Pla / Thai Fish Cake — FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Food Composition Table and HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Food Composition Table; Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Food Composition Table for deep-fried fish paste products and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo database entries for Thai fish cake. Values represent the composite dish including dipping sauce contribution averaged into per-100g figures. All 27 micronutrient fields populated from these two approved primary regional sources.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database — Thai fish cakes (analogous estimate: deep-fried fish paste patties with starch binder, GI ~50–60)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Tod Mun Pla exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on deep-fried fish paste patties with starch binder (GI range ~50–60 reported for similar Southeast Asian fish cake products). GL per serving calculated as: (52 × 13.65g available carbohydrate per 130g serving) / 100 = 7.1. The dipping sauce contributes approximately 8g sugar per 30g sauce, which is included in the carbohydrate total. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content at 620mg per 100g; a standard 130g serving delivers approximately 806mg sodium — over one-third of the 2,000mg daily limit — from fish sauce, fish paste, and red curry paste.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit Tod Mun Pla to 2 pieces per occasion, use the sweet chilli dipping sauce minimally (it contributes an additional 100–200mg sodium), and order as a shared starter rather than a personal portion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycaemic load of 7.1 per 130g serving (GI 52, low category); only 13.65g available carbohydrate per serving, with fish protein and frying fat further blunting post-prandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: A 4-piece serving of Tod Mun Pla is a manageable low-GL appetiser for people with Type 2 Diabetes; the primary glycaemic concern is the sweet chilli dipping sauce (approximately 8g sugar per 30g), which should be used sparingly or replaced with a reduced-sugar variant.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (11.8g/100g) with relatively low saturated fat (2.5g/100g); high sodium (620mg/100g) is the primary cardiovascular concern, while omega-3 fatty acids from Spanish mackerel/threadfin and selenium (28mcg/100g) provide cardioprotective benefit.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease patients, Tod Mun Pla offers beneficial omega-3 fatty acids and selenium from the fish base, but the high sodium content warrants portion control — limit to 2 pieces per occasion and avoid the dipping sauce to keep sodium intake within cardiovascular-safe limits.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High selenium (28mcg/100g; 36mcg per 130g serving — approximately 65% of the RDA), vitamin D (2.5mcg/100g), vitamin B12 (1.8mcg/100g), omega-3 fatty acids from deep-sea fish, and anti-inflammatory compounds (turmeric, galangal, chilli) in red curry paste.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Tod Mun Pla has a strong anti-inflammatory nutritional profile for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to its fish-derived selenium, vitamin D, and omega-3 content combined with anti-inflammatory spices in the red curry paste, making it a clinically beneficial choice despite deep-frying.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tod Mun Pla (ทอดมันปลา, literally 'deep-fried fish meat') is one of Thailand's most iconic appetisers and a staple starter at Thai restaurants worldwide, including Singapore's Thai restaurant and hawker scene. The dish is made by blending freshly minced fish — typically Spanish mackerel (tenggiri) or threadfin, prized for their firm, bouncy texture — with red curry paste, finely sliced kaffir lime leaves (bai makrut, cut into hair-thin ribbons that remain visible in the finished cake), fish sauce, egg, and thin-sliced green beans into a sticky, dense paste. This paste is shaped into flat, round patties approximately 5–6cm in diameter and deep-fried in hot oil until golden-brown and slightly puffed. The exterior is crisp and lightly caramelised; the interior is springy and bouncy — a texture Thais describe as pleasingly elastic. The dish is served with a classic sweet chilli dipping sauce containing thin-sliced cucumber, shallot rings, and crushed peanuts. Nutritionally, Tod Mun Pla is a high-protein (14.5g/100g), moderate-fat (11.8g/100g) dish with a low glycaemic index (GI 52) and low glycaemic load per serving (GL 7.1). It is notably rich in selenium (28mcg/100g — approximately 51% of the RDA), vitamin D (2.5mcg/100g), vitamin B12 (1.8mcg/100g), niacin (5.5mg/100g), and phosphorus (225mg/100g) from its fish base. The red curry paste contributes anti-inflammatory compounds including turmeric, galangal, and chilli. The primary clinical concern is high sodium (620mg/100g; ~806mg per 130g serving) from fish sauce and curry paste, making portion control important for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients. The dish is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Food Composition Table and HPB Singapore Foodo database — both approved primary regional sources for Southeast Asian dishes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database with confidence 0.9. All core macros and 25 of 29 micronutrient fields are populated with numeric values. The dish is well-documented in regional food composition literature.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Tod Mun Pla (deep-fried fish paste patties) is not available in the FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Table or HPB Singapore Foodo database as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish (fish cakes + dipping sauce) is not reported in the available approved sources. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.1g/100g from trace amounts in frying oil per FAO/INFOODS SEA data. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on deep-fried fish paste patties with starch binder (GI range ~50–60); no direct measurement for Tod Mun Pla exists. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as (52 × 13.65g available carbohydrate per 130g serving) / 100 = 7.1. The dipping sauce (30g of the 130g serving) contributes approximately 8g sugar and 100–200mg additional sodium. FoodOn URI left null as no exact concept exists for Thai fish cake; FoodEx2 code A0GPF used as closest parent for deep-fried fish paste products. SNOMED CT code left null to avoid fabrication.
